name: Social Security
description: Monthly benefits for retirees, disabled workers, and survivors. The largest single federal expenditure.

name: Medicare
description: Health insurance for Americans 65+ and certain disabled individuals. Covers hospital stays, doctor visits, and prescriptions.

name: Medicaid & CHIP
description: Health coverage for low-income Americans including children, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled individuals.

name: National Defense
description: Military operations, personnel, weapons systems, and national security. Includes DoD and intelligence agencies.

name: Interest on Debt
description: Payments on the national debt. This mandatory expense has grown significantly as interest rates have risen.

name: Income Security
description: SNAP food assistance, housing aid, unemployment insurance, earned income tax credits, and other safety net programs.

name: Veterans' Affairs
description: Healthcare, education, and benefits for military veterans. Includes VA hospitals and disability compensation.

name: Education
description: Federal student aid, K-12 grants, Head Start, and education research. Most K-12 funding comes from states.

name: Transportation & Infrastructure
description: Highways, bridges, airports, rail, and public transit. Includes the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act spending.

name: Health Research & Public Health
description: NIH research grants, CDC operations, FDA oversight, and disease prevention programs.

name: International Affairs
description: Foreign aid, diplomatic missions, international development, and global health programs. Less than 1% of the budget.

name: All Other
description: Science, energy, environment, agriculture, justice, general government, and other federal activities.
